summary: We are in full launch mode for two flagship programs—Asia Start and Intelligent China—while preparing a joint China Going-Global report with IE Business School for a September launch in Singapore.
summaryDetail: The top priority is executing the Asia Start and Intelligent China program launches: Asia Start is running two parallel campaigns (AI-driven and traditional) for the first month, and the Intelligent China website goes live this week, with SEO efforts concentrated on these two programs over US partnerships. Urgent this week: finalize the Intelligent China webpage, launch the Asia Start campaigns, and complete the English translation of the joint report with IE Business School, as we target a mid-September launch at Tech in Asia or Tyson events in Singapore. Meanwhile, the team is managing brochure updates, press release adjustments for Shun Tian Shu, and agency prioritization (Media Minds over new searches). Upcoming: we will begin SCM campaigns for Intelligent China, explore op-ed contributions from leadership, and audit conversion tracking to support SEO strategy.

meetings.title: SEO & Program Prioritization Strategy
meetings.summary: Team discusses SEO strategy to improve search rankings for programs like Asia Star and Intelligent China, prioritizing these two programs over US partnerships. They plan to audit current conversion tracking and analyze existing participant profiles.

meetings.title: Marketing Team Meeting
meetings.summary: Team discussed feedback on menu categorization, brochure updates, social media agency search, and IE report translation. Decisions made on Shun Tian Shu press release approach and agency prioritization.

meetings.title: New Chinese Playbook for Going Global Report
meetings.summary: Team discusses jointly publishing a report on Chinese companies globalizing to Southeast Asia, with contributions from both schools. They plan to launch at Tech in Asia or Tyson event in Singapore in mid-September.

meetings.title: Asia Start & New Program Planning
meetings.summary: Discussed Asia Start campaign with two routes (AI vs traditional) and introduced a new in-depth program 'Intelligent China' targeting foreigners, with modules in Beijing, Shanghai/Hangzhou, and Shenzhen.

meetings.title: China-Europe Cooperation Discussion
meetings.summary: Discussion on China-Europe relations, focusing on geopolitical tensions, de-risking, and the role of companies versus governments in market operations.

decisions.text: Prioritize Asia Star and Intelligent China programs over US partnerships for initial SEO focus.
Intelligent China webpage to launch this week; will be key for driving traffic.
Only one press release for Shun Tian Shu, either incorporating 10 points or turning into 10-point article.
Prioritize Media Minds agency over searching for new ones due to small budget and time constraints.
Report will be jointly published by CKGSB and IE Business School.

decisions.text: Report will include a main research feature, op-eds, and industry case studies.

decisions.text: Target launch at events in Singapore in mid-September (Tech in Asia or Tyson).
decisions.meetingTitle: New Chinese Playbook for Going Global Report

decisions.text: Asia Start will run two parallel campaigns for the first month: AI-driven (Joseph) and traditional (Daisy).
Intelligent China program will be marketed as a deeper dive compared to Asia Start to avoid cannibalization.
Republish Professor Edward Tse's article as a Professor Analysis article on Knowledge, mentioning original source
